previous_results_for_reference.label: R5 — LFSR tie-break, ETR-unit RTP scale, still PC-keyed STT

unblock_plan_applied: Lengthened NUM_OPS from 2000 to 100000 (10000-op warmup + 90000-op measurement window).
Added LFSR-driven random tie-break in `find_victim` (parameter TIE_BREAK_LFSR_SEED=0xACE1) so all-MAX_ETR initial state no longer collapses to way 0.
Scaled reuse-distance delta from global-access units to ETR-time-units (delta >> ETR_W) before writing into the per-PC RTP, so 16-op hot-PC reuse distances no longer saturate to MAX_ETR.

Rekeyed the Sampled Cache Tracker (STT) from `(pc, set_id)` to `(set_id, tag)` per the HPCA'22 paper section 4. Added `parameter TAG_W` and `input acc_tag` to `e1_mockingjay_prod`; the STT entry now stores `{valid, set_id, tag, ts}` (PC dropped from entry). The RTP remains indexed by the demand PC `acc_pc`; the reuse distance fed into it is observed via the line-address-keyed STT, so a scan PC that walks distinct tags no longer registers as a same-PC STT hit with a 1-op reuse distance.
unblock_plan_outcome: PASS. With line-address STT keying, the scan PC `0x20_0000` no longer registers spurious 1-op reuse distances against the STT; the PC-keyed RTP learns the hot-set PCs as short-reuse (insert with low ETR, keep) and the scan PC stays unlearned, so scan lines are inserted at the conservative mid-range ETR and the tie-broken victim selection lands on scan lines first. Mockingjay measurement-window hit-rate improves from 22.15% (R5) to 58.78%, a +36.6 percentage point absolute and +165 % relative jump from R5; vs LRU the policy now leads by +8.76 percentage points absolute (+17.50 % relative), clearing the +10 % relative pass threshold.

root_cause_history.root_cause: Deterministic argmax collapsed victim selection onto way 0 at reset; reuse-distance fed into RTP in global-access units instead of ETR units, saturating fast paths to MAX_ETR; NUM_OPS=2000 too short to warm STT/RTP.

STT keyed by `(pc, set_id)`. The scan PC `0x20_0000` was reused across every scan tag at the same set, so the second scan op matched the STT and reported a ~1-global-op reuse distance; the PC-keyed RTP then learned scan_pc as short-reuse, scan lines were inserted with ETR=0 (most-kept), and they evicted the hot lines.

root_cause_history.resolution: Rekey STT by line address (set_id, tag). Scan accesses are now STT misses (fresh tag at the same set), so scan_pc's RTP entry is not updated with false short-reuse signals. Hot accesses (same tag reused) remain STT hits and feed the hot PCs' RTP with their true short reuse distance.

rtl_changes: Added 16-bit Galois LFSR `tie_lfsr_q` (polynomial x^16+x^14+x^13+x^11+1) advanced each acc_valid cycle.
Rewrote `find_victim` to do a 3-pass argmax: find max ETR, count ways tied at max, pick the (rnd mod tied_count)-th tied way.
Scaled `delta` to ETR-time-units (delta >> ETR_W) in the RTP update path; saturate to MAX_ETR.
Added module-header documentation of the tie-break randomization rationale.
Added `e1_mockingjay_prod.sv` to scripts/run_rtl_check.sh per-module verilator-strict lint loop; lints clean.
Added parameter TAG_W (default 36) and input port `acc_tag` to `e1_mockingjay_prod`.
Changed `stt_entry_t` from `{valid, pc, set_id, ts}` to `{valid, set_id, tag, ts}` — removed PC from the STT entry.
Updated `stt_lookup` to key on `(set_id, tag)` instead of `(pc, set_id)`.
Updated the STT allocation path to record `acc_tag` (with `acc_set`); RTP still indexed and matched by `acc_pc`.
test_changes: Measurement counters split by warmup vs measure window; LRU counters snapshotted at the warmup boundary so both policies are compared over the same window.
Updated `MOCKINGJAY_VS_LRU_SUMMARY` summary line to include warmup_ops and measure_ops.
Added `acc_tag` input to `e1_mockingjay_prod_tb` (parameter TAG_W=24) and threaded it through to `u_mj`.
cocotb harness drives `dut.acc_tag` from the stream's tag value (masked to TAG_W bits) on every access; reset clears it.
phone_class_claim_blocked_reason: cocotb synthetic stream is not phone-class; remains blocked until full-system-simulator or silicon evidence.
